title: Experimental Verification of Multicopy Activation of Genuine Multipartite Entanglement
summary: A central concept in quantum information processing is genuine multipartite entanglement (GME), a type of correlation beyond biseparability, that is, correlations that cannot be explained by statistical mixtures of partially separable states. GME is relevant for characterizing and benchmarking compl…

title: Time-domain Measurement of Auger Electron Dynamics in Xenon and Krypton Atoms after Giant Resonance Photoionization
summary: Attosecond soft-X-ray pump-probe spectroscopy of xenon and krypton atoms reveals two previously unobserved dynamical features in xenon that are inconsistent with lifetimes inferred from energy-domain measurements.

title: Phase-Dependent Squeezing in Dual-Comb Interferometry
summary: Manipulating the quantum noise of continuous-wave lasers through squeezing has reshaped optical interferometry. However, progress in optical frequency comb interferometry with pulsed squeezed sources has been limited, despite the role of frequency combs in ultraprecise optical metrology. Here, we in…

title: Quantum Signatures of Proper Time in Optical Ion Clocks
summary: High-precision clocks based on quantum systems will work in a regime where a quantum description of proper time might be necessary.

title: Compatible Instability: Gauge Constraints of Elasticity Inherited by Electronic Nematic Criticality
summary: A 19th-century theory of elasticity inspires a new way to analyze a quantum phase transition that has become central to modern quantum materials research.

title: Asymmetric ultrafast carrier relaxation in $β\text{−}{\mathrm{TeO}}_{2}$: Phonon bottleneck driven electron-hole imbalance
summary: $β\text{−}{\text{TeO}}_{2}$ has emerged as a promising wide-band-gap semiconductor for next-generation optoelectronic devices, owing to its exceptionally high hole mobility and excellent nonlinear optical properties. title: Carrier compensation and quantum interference effects near room temperature in a Heusler ferromagnet
summary: We have conducted magnetotransport measurements on samples of NiFeMnAl and $\mathrm{NiF}{\mathrm{e}}_{1.25}{\mathrm{Mn}}_{0.75}\mathrm{Al}$, both of which are ferromagnetic.
